title: 4. The Pianist (2002) – Dir. Roman Polanski
content:
4. The Pianist (2002) – Dir. Roman Polanski
• Biographical Drama
• Themes: Survival, resilience, the relationship between suffering and art.
• Relevance: While different in setting (WWII), The Pianist shows how discipline and survival are deeply intertwined, much like Vagabond and Whiplash.
5. Amadeus (1984) – Dir. Miloš Forman
• Historical Biopic / Musical Drama
• Themes: Jealousy, the burden of genius, mentorship as rivalry.
• Relevance: Explores toxic mentorship and competition in music, akin to Whiplash and Real, but from the perspective of an embittered, less-talented rival (Salieri).
6. Rocky (1976) – Dir. John G. Avildsen
• Sports Drama
• Themes: Endurance, self-worth, mentorship, underdog narrative.
• Relevance: Rocky introduces the mentor-student bond, the struggle to prove oneself, and the grit of physical and emotional endurance, mirroring Real and Vagabond’s underdog themes.

Which Film to Choose?
• If you want a martial-arts-meets-Whiplash angle → Raging Bull
• If you want psychological horror tied to ambition → Black Swan
context: From document: Analyzing the Common Threads in Whiplash, Real, and Vagabond — chunk 9 (chunk 9/14)
